macro.title: A faster disaster payout model for U.S. hazard insurance
macro.what: In the first half of this two-part topic, I described the piecemeal nature of private hazard insurance policies, state-sponsored “last resort” programs, the National Flood Insurance Program, and federal aid through emergency declarations
macro.why: Rates & inflation shape discount rates and housing affordability.
macro.market: Lower rates support demand/valuations; higher rates pressure builders/REITs and slow transactions.

macro.title: Former Fed chair Alan Greenspan dies at 100
macro.what: Economist Alan Greenspan, the former long-time Federal Reserve chairman whose policies are tied to the 2008 housing crisis, died at his home on Monday due to complications of Parkinson’s disease

housing.title: MBA white paper warns housing supply may outpace demand
housing.what: MBA paper says slowing household formation and steady construction could outpace demand and pressure home prices in some markets.
housing.why: Price trend signals wealth effects and buyer/seller power.
housing.market: Firm prices help builder margins; softness hits comps and sentiment.

housing.title: Better stories, less hype: How the reverse mortgage industry can improve public perception
housing.what: Reverse mortgage perception improves when originators share verified stories, explain HECM rules clearly and avoid hype.
housing.why: Rates & inflation shape discount rates and housing affordability.
housing.market: Lower rates support demand/valuations; higher rates pressure builders/REITs and slow transactions.

housing.title: Georgia developers back push for permit-to-plat shot clocks
housing.what: Georgia residential land developers succeeded this year at convincing lawmakers that the permitting timeline needed to be faster and more predictable to dent the state’s growing housing imbalances
housing.why: Supply pipeline sets future inventory and construction activity.
housing.market: Stronger pipeline aids suppliers; oversupply later can weigh on pricing.

housing.title: Florida Dominates May’s Most Expensive Home Sales, Led by a $75 Million Boca Raton Deal

housing.market: Rising inventory/DOM tilts to buyers; tight supply supports prices.
